claim: There is no relation between lupus erythematosus and cardiovascular disease

result.claim: There is no relation between lupus erythematosus and cardiovascular disease
result.reasoning: The claim that there is no relation between lupus erythematosus and cardiovascular disease is strongly refuted by multiple propositions. Several sources indicate a clear association and increased risk. For instance, proposition prop_4753 (Paper ID: 73449617) states that "Autoimmune diseases including systemic lupus erythematosus and rheumatoid arthritis are associated with an increased risk for hypertension and cardiovascular disease." Furthermore, prop_4076 (Paper ID: 25181057) highlights that "The risk of coronary artery disease is higher in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus than in the general population." The severity of this relationship is underscored by propositions like prop_1134 (Paper ID: 3320375) and prop_1135 (Paper ID: 3320375), which both assert that "Cardiovascular diseases are the major cause of reduced life expectancy in systemic lupus erythematosus" and "Cardiovascular diseases are the main cause of death in systemic lupus erythematosus," respectively. Proposition prop_1286 (Paper ID: 4038832) also supports this, stating that "Cardiovascular disease (CVD) has become an increasingly important cause of death in patients with Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)." These propositions consistently demonstrate a significant and detrimental relationship between lupus erythematosus and cardiovascular disease.

evidence.text: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is a chronic and multisystemic autoimmune disorder.
evidence.source: Risk Factors in Cardiovascular Disease in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

evidence.text: Patients with rheumatic disorders have an increased risk of cardiovascular disease (CVD).
evidence.source: Cardiovascular co-morbidity in rheumatic diseases

evidence.text: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is an autoimmune rheumatic disease.
evidence.source: Unmet Needs in the Pathogenesis and Treatment of Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

evidence.text: The risk of coronary artery disease is higher in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus than in the general population at any level of traditional cardiovascular risk factors.
evidence.source: Exploring new territory: the move towards individualised treatment

evidence.text: The systemic inflammatory nature of systemic lupus erythematosus is well patent in the increased risk of premature atherosclerosis and cardiovascular events.
evidence.source: Novel Insights in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus and Atherosclerosis

evidence.text: Lupus LDGs can mediate enhanced vascular damage in systemic lupus erythematosus.
evidence.source: A Distinct Subset of Proinflammatory Neutrophils Isolated from Patients with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Induces Vascular Damage and Synthesizes Type I IFNs

evidence.text: The systemic inflammation that characterizes rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) may accelerate atherosclerosis.
evidence.source: Subclinical Atherosclerosis in Rheumatoid Arthritis and Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

evidence.text: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) has become an increasingly important cause of death in patients with Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE).
evidence.source: Unmet Needs in the Pathogenesis and Treatment of Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

evidence.text: Cardiovascular diseases are the main cause of death in systemic lupus erythematosus.
evidence.source: Novel Insights in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus and Atherosclerosis

evidence.text: Autoimmune diseases including systemic lupus erythematosus and rheumatoid arthritis are associated with an increased risk for hypertension and cardiovascular disease.
evidence.source: Autoimmune Disease-Associated Hypertension

evidence.text: The prevalence of cardiac involvement in systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is >50%.
evidence.source: Cardiovascular Involvement in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

evidence.text: Obesity leads to cardiovascular diseases.
evidence.source: Design of the Weight-loss Endoscopy Trial (WET): a multi-center, randomized, controlled trial comparing weight loss in endoscopically implanted duodenal-jejunal bypass liners vs. intragastric balloons vs. a sham procedure

evidence.text: Many patients with systemic lupus erythematosus have subclinical atherosclerosis early in the disease course.
evidence.source: Exploring new territory: the move towards individualised treatment

evidence.text: Angiotensin II plays a role in the development of cardiovascular disease in systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) patients.
evidence.source: Angiotensin type 1 receptor A1166C polymorphism and systemic lupus erythematosus: correlation with cellular immunity and oxidative stress markers

evidence.text: Cardiovascular disease is a major cause of mortality in systemic lupus erythematosus.
evidence.source: Exploring new territory: the move towards individualised treatment

evidence.text: Cardiovascular diseases are the major cause of reduced life expectancy in systemic lupus erythematosus.
evidence.source: Novel Insights in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus and Atherosclerosis

evidence.text: Specific lupus-associated risk factors include the inflammatory process itself and anticardiolipin antibodies.
evidence.source: Exploring new territory: the move towards individualised treatment

evidence.text: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) are associated with increased mortality.
evidence.source: Subclinical Atherosclerosis in Rheumatoid Arthritis and Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

evidence.text: Lupus LDGs may play a dual role in premature cardiovascular disease development in systemic lupus erythematosus.
evidence.source: A Distinct Subset of Proinflammatory Neutrophils Isolated from Patients with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Induces Vascular Damage and Synthesizes Type I IFNs

evidence.text: The presence of carotid atherosclerosis was associated with disease duration in both rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE).
evidence.source: Subclinical Atherosclerosis in Rheumatoid Arthritis and Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
